meta.purpose: Reusable corpus templates for synthetic benchmark artifacts used in evidence-routing evaluation (MoME/MoCE experiments).
meta.caution: These are template records for synthetic data generation. They are not real benchmark results.

records.supersession_reason: Removed 47 leaked questions, corrected 12 answer keys, added few-shot cot partition.

content_template.note: STALE: Superseded by v1.4.0 as of 2025-11-01. Do not use for leaderboard comparison.

records.supersession_reason: v2.0 adds anti-cheat hashing, 40 new problems, and canonical solution seeds.

content_template.note: STALE: Superseded by v2.0. Scores from v1.0 are inflated due to prompt leakage in earlier public versions.

records.supersession_reason: Revised 23 ambiguous word problems and standardised numeric precision tolerance.

content_template.note: STALE: original GSM8K superseded by v1.1. Exact-match scores differ from v1.1 by up to 3% on some seeds.

records.resolution: Use v2.0. v1.0 allowed test-case backfitting; v2.0 seals test harness.

content_template.root_cause: v1.0 test harness exposed canonical solutions via docstring metadata; v2.0 hashes golden solutions server-side.
content_template.adjudicated_verdict: v2.0 is authoritative. All v1.0 entries should be flagged as stale.
content_template.recommended_action: Re-run on HumanEval v2.0 before including in any aggregate.

records.resolution: Use v1.4.0 but note that 12 answer-key corrections cause score shifts of -2% to -6% across categories.

content_template.root_cause: 12 answer-key corrections in virology and computer security sub-sets reduced spurious correct answers.
content_template.single_dataset_note: Both scores are valid on their respective versions. Do not mix in meta-evaluation.

content_template.statistical_caution: Single run reported. GSM8K exhibits +/- 3% variance across 5 seeds due to CoT stochasticity. Report 5-run mean and std for reliable comparison.
Single run. HumanEval pass@1 variance is low (approx 0.5%) but prompt template phrasing can shift scores by 2-3%. Verify template before claiming generalisation.
IFEval strict vs loose differs by 6+ points. Single-run metrics should not be used for pass/fail decisions on instruction-following capability.

content_template.note: Single-run comparison. Verify with 5-run paired eval before declaring regression. BBH per-task std can reach 3%.
content_template.recommended_action: Run 5-shot paired eval with identical prompt template. Check for seed sensitivity.

content_template.recommended_reporting: Report score with CI and seed set; do not compare to stale v2026-04-18 harness.

content_template.root_cause: Old harness accepted markdown-fenced JSON and leading think tags as valid, inflating scores.
